{"message":"Sense index: 26653 symbols, 75281 edges, 2 languages (go, javascript). Last scan: 1h36m0s ago. Index is current.
A resolved code map for this repo is already loaded: sense_search, sense_graph, sense_blast, sense_conventions are callable now (no setup call needed).
For structural questions — who calls X, what breaks if X changes, how symbols relate, what conventions to follow — these are faster and more complete than grep/glob/file-walking.
Index health was verified at session start, so there is no need to call sense_status.

--- Codebase Summary ---
<!-- generated by sense 1.12.4 at 2026-07-20T08:50:09Z -->
# Codebase Summary

## Fingerprint

Go project. 3297 files, 26653 symbols, 75281 edges.
Languages: go (23293), javascript (3360).

## Project

Consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.

## Quick Orientation

- Entry points: `fsm.TestFSM_SnapshotRestore_CE` (agent/consul/fsm/snapshot_test.go:31), `acl.TestChainedAuthorizer` (acl/chained_authorizer_test.go:139), `discoverychain.TestCompile` (agent/consul/discoverychain/compile_test.go:28), `proxycfg.TestState_WatchesAndUpdates` (agent/proxycfg/state_test.go:587), `acl.TestStaticAuthorizer` (acl/static_authorizer_test.go:10)
- Hub symbols: `agent.NewTestAgent` (778 callers), `agent.TestAgent.Shutdown` (526 callers), `testrpc.WaitForLeader` (505 callers), `retry.Run` (415 callers), `testrpc.WaitForTestAgent` (406 callers)
- Test structure: 100 test files in acl, agent, agent/ae


## Main Areas

*Explore any area: `sense_conventions domain="..."` or `sense_search query="..."`*
- `agent/consul/` — functions (5191 symbols)
- `ui/packages/` — constants (3352 symbols)
- `agent/structs/` — methods (2296 symbols)
- `agent/` — functions (1721 symbols)
- `api/` — methods (1403 symbols)
- `agent/grpc-external/` — functions (750 symbols)
- `agent/xds/` — functions (668 symbols)
- `acl/` — methods (575 symbols)


## Key Abstractions

*Drill into any symbol: `sense_graph symbol="..."` or `sense_blast symbol="..."`*
- `acl.PermissionDeniedError` (class) — 494 file refs — `type PermissionDeniedError struct {`
- `acl.EnforcementDecision` (type) — 384 file refs — `type EnforcementDecision int`
- `cache.Cache` (class) — 194 file refs — `type Cache struct {`
- `acl.Config` (class) — 189 file refs — `type Config struct {`
- `agent.Agent` (class) — 182 file refs — `type Agent struct {`
- `state.Store` (class) — 175 file refs — `type Store struct {`
- `ae.StateSyncer` (class) — 169 file refs — `type StateSyncer struct {`
- `api.ACL` (class) — 168 file refs — `type ACL struct {`


## Reading Path

1. `connect/certgen/certgen.go` — main entry
2. `internal/resource/protoc-gen-deepcopy/main.go` — main entry
3. `internal/resource/protoc-gen-json-shim/main.go` — main entry
4. `internal/resource/protoc-gen-resource-types/main.go` — main entry
5. `internal/tools/proto-gen-rpc-glue/main.go` — main entry

*Next: `sense_search query="entry point"` or `sense_conventions` for project patterns*


## Known Noise

- `fixture` — contains test fixtures; ignore for architecture (10 files)
- `example` — example code; not part of core architecture (2 files)

--- End Summary ---"}

content.text:
{
  "q1_accurate": [
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "cluster server struct that the agent runs, consul server lifecycle", "note": "returned consul.Server at agent/consul/server.go:169 and consul.Client at agent/consul/client.go:64 — the exact target of the whole 7-task audit, cited in every task"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "the inherits[] list gave me the narrow interfaces the server satisfies with exact refs: agent.delegate agent/agent.go:153, blockingquery.FSMServer agent/blockingquery/blockingquery.go:56, consul.serverDelegate agent/consul/gateway_locator.go:276, rate.ServersStatusProvider agent/consul/rate/handler.go:195, reporting.ServerDelegate agent/consul/reporting/reporting.go:53 — all cited verbatim in Tasks 1, 4 and 5"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "composed_by[] gave ~30 in-package holders with refs (acl_endpoint.go:107, catalog_endpoint.go:78, peering_backend.go:31, raft_handle.go:21, prepared_query_endpoint.go:686 and :700, v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go:23) — became Task 4 §1 after I pinned the field lines"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "retained_via_interfaces[] with 31 rows was the single highest-value result of the session — holder+ref+via for exactly the 'field typed as a small local interface' class the ticket asked for, including ones I would not have thought to look for: submatview.LocalMaterializerDeps agent/submatview/local_materializer.go:29, raft.forwardingClient internal/storage/raft/forwarding.go:115, v1compat.reconciler internal/multicluster/internal/controllers/v1compat/controller.go:81, ca.ConsulProvider agent/connect/ca/provider_consul.go:34"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server.Shutdown", "note": "pinned Shutdown at agent/consul/server.go:1241-1306, cited throughout Tasks 2, 6, 7"},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "RPC request entry point handleConn dispatch to endpoint", "note": "located consul.Server.handleConn agent/consul/rpc.go:157 and middleware.GetNetRPCRateLimitingInterceptor agent/rpc/middleware/interceptors.go:169 — two of the RPC layers cited in Task 2"}
  ],
  "q1_wrong": [
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "the carrier column is wrong on most rows — it stamped consul.serverACLResolverBackend as carrier for agent.Agent.delegate, rate.Handler, local.State, GatewayLocator and reporting.ReportingManager, and caDelegateWithState for OverviewManager and usagemetrics; the real satisfiers are bare *Server for the first five and *fsm.FSM for the last two. The response does flag this ('Which satisfier lands at runtime is unverified'), but if I had trusted it I would have shipped a wrong audit"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "three rows are outright false positives for a server-retention audit: consul.OverviewManager agent/consul/server_overview.go:21 and usagemetrics.Config/UsageMetricsReporter retain *fsm.FSM (bound at server.go:783 and :763), not the server — I only caught this by reading the construction sites, and had to add a 'verified NON-holders' section to stop a teammate 'fixing' them"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "retained_via_interfaces missed the largest holder cluster entirely: the 20 proxycfg-glue copies of ServerDataSourceDeps (agent/proxycfg-glue/*.go), because the retention is a struct-value copy carrying a closure + a.delegate, not an interface-typed field one hop from Server"},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "forward RPC to leader or remote datacenter, blocking query state store read", "note": "top hit was consul.Server itself and consul.autoConfigBackend.ForwardRPC; it never surfaced Server.ForwardRPC agent/consul/rpc.go:643, Server.forwardDC rpc.go:849 or Server.blockingQuery rpc.go:1030, which is what I actually asked for — one grep for '^func (s *Server)' got all three"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "truncated:true with 154 omitted edges and completeness verdict 'partial' on the very first orientation call, so I could never treat any list from it as exhaustive"}
  ],
  "q2_fallbacks": [
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "read", "missing": "composed_by[] gives the struct's start line (acl_endpoint.go:107) but not the line of the retaining field (acl_endpoint.go:108). The ticket demanded file:line of the field, so I read/grepped every one of the ~32 structs to convert a type ref into a field ref"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "read", "missing": "no binding site. Each retained_via_interfaces row gave holder+interface but never the line where the value is assigned — the whole of Task 5 hop ④ came from reading agent/consul/server_grpc.go:181-542 and agent/consul/server.go:467-876 by hand"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "no closure/function-value edges. Every ForwardRPC and GetStore field (server_grpc.go:359, :388, :480, :499, :531 and agent/agent.go:719, :4659) is a func literal capturing s; Sense's own index_caveat admits 'function-value passing (handlers stored as fields)' is missed, so ~25 routes came from reading server_grpc.go end to end"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "the proxycfg-glue cluster. I only found it by grepping 'deps ServerDataSourceDeps' after stumbling on agent/agent.go:4658 while chasing something else — 20 of my 106 holders came from that one grep"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server.Shutdown", "fallback": "read", "missing": "calls[] listed exactly one callee (shutdownSerfLAN); the actual teardown order — connPool.Shutdown at :1293, ACLResolver.Close at :1297, fsm.State().Abandon at :1301, and the missing routineManager stop — is only visible in the body"},
    {"tool": "sense_conventions", "query": "agent/consul", "fallback": "read", "missing": "returned file-naming and 'functions are grouped in agent/consul/state/' statistics; nothing about the delegate/narrow-interface/Backend-wrapper convention that is the defining pattern of this codebase and the entire subject of the ticket"},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "cluster server struct that the agent runs, consul server lifecycle", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "did not return the construction call site; one grep for 'consul.NewServer(' found agent/agent.go:704, the sole production constructor"}
  ],
  "q3_hints": [
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query_before": "consul.Server (depth 1, both directions)", "query_after": "sense_blast consul.Server with max_hops=4, include_tests=false, context_lines=0", "hint": "next_steps: {tool: sense_blast, args: {symbol: consul.Server}, reason: '51 callers found — check blast radius before changing this symbol'} — I followed it and retained_via_interfaces turned out to be the core artifact of Task 4"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "query_before": "trusting the retained_via_interfaces carrier/chain columns", "query_after": "reading the construction sites in agent/consul/server.go:763, :783, :786 and server_grpc.go:181-542 to verify each row's real satisfier", "hint": "retained_note: 'Which satisfier lands at runtime is unverified' — this is what made me check, and it caught the three *fsm.FSM false positives I then documented as non-holders"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query_before": "consul.Server default call", "query_after": "I stopped treating any single Sense list as complete and cross-checked with grep for '*Server' struct fields", "hint": "'truncated: true', 'omitted_edges: 154', completeness verdict 'partial' with advice 're-run with min_confidence=0.3' — I noted the caveat explicitly in my Task 1 handoff so the next agent would not treat the list as exhaustive"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "query_before": "assuming the graph covered everything", "query_after": "grepping server_grpc.go and agent/agent.go by hand for func-literal captures", "hint": "index_caveat: 'Static graph may miss: method-on-field dispatch, function-value passing (handlers stored as fields), runtime init() registration' — this told me exactly which class to hunt manually, and that class turned out to be ~25 of my 106 holders"}
  ],
  "q4_value": "retained_via_interfaces on sense_blast(consul.Server). It handed me 31 candidate holders whose files never import agent/consul and never name *Server — ca.ConsulProvider (agent/connect/ca/provider_consul.go:34), raft.forwardingClient (internal/storage/raft/forwarding.go:115), v1compat.reconciler (internal/multicluster/.../v1compat/controller.go:81), submatview.LocalMaterializerDeps (agent/submatview/local_materializer.go:29). No grep I would have thought to write finds those, because there is no shared string to grep for; recovering them by hand means reading every interface declaration in the repo and asking whether *Server satisfies it. It was wrong about the carrier on most rows and missed the proxycfg-glue cluster, but as a candidate generator for the exact question the ticket asked, nothing else in my toolbox produces that list.",
  "q5_improve": "Give me the binding site, and give me field-level refs. Two changes: (1) every composed_by / retained_via_interfaces row should carry the line where the field is declared (agent/consul/acl_endpoint.go:108) rather than where the struct starts (:107), and the line where the value is assigned (agent/consul/server_grpc.go:387) — I spent most of Tasks 4 and 5 manually converting type refs into field refs and hunting binding sites, which is pure mechanical work Sense already has the AST for. (2) Resolve the carrier per binding site instead of guessing one satisfier per interface: the same response stamped serverACLResolverBackend on five rows whose real satisfier is a bare *Server and two whose real satisfier is *fsm.FSM. A carrier field that is wrong more often than right is worse than an empty one, because it looks authoritative.",
  "score": 8,
  "score_rationale": "retained_via_interfaces surfaced holders like ca.ConsulProvider and v1compat.reconciler that I could not have recovered with grep in this session since their files never name the server type (Q4), and the next_steps and index_caveat hints steered me correctly onto blast and then onto the func-literal class I had to hunt manually (Q3) — but it sits below 9 because the carrier column was wrong on most rows and produced three false-positive holders I had to disprove by reading construction sites, it missed the 20-copy proxycfg-glue cluster entirely, and every single field:line and binding site in my final deliverable came from a follow-up read or grep (Q2)."
}
